subject: NovaPulse Follow-Up — SOC 2 Report, API Docs & Next Steps
body:
Hi Sarah,

Great speaking with you earlier today — really appreciate you walking me through your current reporting setup and where the pain points are. The fragmented tooling situation (Tableau + legacy BI + Salesforce + spreadsheets) is something we see constantly, and the 30–40 hours/week reconciliation burden is unfortunately very typical for a team your size.

As promised, here's the follow-up:

1. SOC 2 Type II Report
Attached under NDA. This covers Security, Availability, Confidentiality, and Processing Integrity — last audited March 2025.

2. API Documentation
Live GraphQL playground and full OpenAPI spec: https://docs.novapulse.io/api
Your dev team can explore this ahead of any technical deep-dive.

3. Technical Overview Document
Attached. This covers all eight modules, deployment architecture, and security posture — should give your IT Director the detail he'll want.

Next step: You mentioned getting your IT Director and BI Lead on a call to go deeper. I'd suggest we do a live demo with a retail-specific use case rather than generic sample data — it'll be much more useful for your team to see something that maps to your actual workflows.

Let me know what times work next week and I'll send a calendar invite.

Best,
Ryan Mitchell
Account Executive | NovaPulse
+1 (415) 555-0172

subject: Re: NovaPulse Follow-Up — SOC 2 Report, API Docs & Next Steps
body:
Hi Ryan,

Thanks for getting this over so quickly — I've forwarded the SOC 2 report to our compliance team and they're reviewing now.

I've looped in David Wong (IT Director) on this thread. He's available Thursday 10am or Friday 2pm PT. Priya (copied) runs our BI team and will join as well.

For the demo, can you show us:
1. The Salesforce bi-directional sync in action
2. How the real-time dashboard refresh actually works
3. The workflow automation builder — we have a manual weekly report that goes to 12 regional managers that I'd love to automate

Also, quick question from David: what's your average implementation timeline for a company our size? We've been burned before by vendors who promised 4 weeks and delivered in 4 months.

Thanks,
Sarah

Sarah Chen
Director of Operations | Brightway Retail

subject: Re: NovaPulse Follow-Up — SOC 2 Report, API Docs & Next Steps
body:
Hi Sarah, David, Priya,

Thursday 10am PT works perfectly — calendar invite coming shortly.

I'll structure the demo around exactly those three use cases:
- Salesforce sync (I'll show both push and pull, plus a webhook-triggered automation)
- Real-time dashboard refresh (sub-2-second via WebSocket — I'll have you watch the data change live)
- Workflow automation for your regional manager report (we'll build a simplified version together so you can see the no-code builder in action)

On implementation timeline: For a 180-person team on cloud-hosted Business plan, you're looking at 3–5 weeks from contract signature to full go-live. That breaks down roughly as:
- Week 1: Tenant provisioning, SSO configuration, initial data migration
- Week 2: CRM connector setup, dashboard configuration for pilot users
- Week 3: Workflow automation build-out, user training
- Weeks 4–5: Pilot rollout, feedback iteration, full launch

We don't charge setup fees, and standard data migration up to 50k records is included. The 3–5 week timeline assumes your team has reasonable availability for configuration decisions — the main variable is usually how quickly you can get us the right stakeholders for SSO setup and data mapping.

Happy to go deeper on this Thursday. Looking forward to it.

Best,
Ryan

subject: NovaPulse Demo Recap + Formal Proposal
body:
Hi Sarah, David, Priya,

Thanks for a great session this morning — really enjoyed the questions from David on the API rate limiting and from Priya on the embedded analytics SDK.

I've looped in Anita (CFO) as Sarah mentioned she'd need to review the commercial terms.

Demo Recap:
- Salesforce bi-directional sync: Demonstrated real-time field mapping and showed the webhook trigger for automatic dashboard refresh on opportunity stage changes
- Dashboard refresh: David clocked it at 1.4 seconds on the complex retail KPI dashboard — confirmed this is via WebSocket, not polling
- Workflow automation: Built the regional manager report distribution workflow together — scheduled trigger every Monday 6am, conditional branching by region, email delivery with PDF attachment

Proposal Attached:
- Plan: Business
- Users: 180
- Annual billing: $45/user/month × 180 × 12 = $97,200/year
- Includes: 2 TB storage, 500k API calls/month, 2 IdPs for SSO, white-labelling, 99.95% uptime SLA, 24/7 phone support

Next Steps:
1. Legal/procurement review of MSA (attached)
2. If you'd like, we can start a 30-day PoC with 10 pilot users and your actual data — this is free and gives you a real answer on fit before committing
3. Target go-live: April 28 if we sign by end of March

Let me know if you have questions or if there's anything I can clarify for Anita.

Best,
Ryan
